skill.name: image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI images through the Image Skill hosted runtime using documented CLI commands, recoverable jobs, hosted media URLs, cost limits, and optional payment flow.
skill.summary: Generate durable hosted AI images from text prompts using Image Skill CLI commands.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: Static external command, network, and filesystem findings are mostly legitimate instructions for this hosted image generation skill. The confirmed risk is that users are asked to run remote npx packages and route generation through an external hosted API, so publication is acceptable only with clear warnings. The weak cryptography matches at SKILL.md lines 3 and 19 are false positives; no cryptographic implementation or prompt injection text was found.

medium_findings.title: Remote package execution through npx
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run npx commands for image-skill and skills installation. This is a legitimate workflow, but it executes remotely fetched packages and creates supply chain exposure if the package, registry, or install path is compromised.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and central to the workflow. The risk is supply chain execution rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Hosted API and spending workflow dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill sends image-generation work to image-skill.com and api.image-skill.com and references credit balance, wallet, payment loop, cost receipts, and media spend. This external service dependency is disclosed, but users should treat prompts, generated media, tokens, and spending controls as third-party operational risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hosted URLs and payment-related workflow are directly documented. No evidence shows secret exfiltration or hidden endpoints.

low_findings.title: Agent home directory modification instructions
low_findings.description: The skill includes fallback commands that set HOME and CODEX_HOME to workspace hidden directories before installing the skill. This is disclosed and scoped, but it can change where agent configuration and skill state are written during setup.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes are explicit and appear intended to handle read-only global paths. The commands do not show deletion, broad file reads, or credential harvesting.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography scanner matches are false positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at the description and token description lines. I found no cryptographic code, hashing call, or encryption workflow in those lines, so these hits do not represent a real crypto vulnerability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain frontmatter prose only. There is no implementation of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar weak crypto.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx package execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Commands use npx with remote packages, including image-skill@latest and skills installation commands. Pinning versions and reviewing package provenance would reduce supply chain risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is present in multiple documented setup and runtime examples. It is risky because it executes downloaded code, not because malicious intent is proven.

dangerous_patterns.title: External hosted service calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs users to hosted image-skill endpoints for documentation, contracts, and API access. This is expected for the product, but it means prompts and outputs may leave the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are explicit and match the skill purpose. No evidence found of undisclosed network destinations.

content.user_title: Generate hosted AI images from prompts
content.value_statement: Agents often need image output without managing model providers, local runtimes, or billing setup. This skill provides a hosted CLI workflow with recoverable jobs, media URLs, cost controls, and receipts.
actual_capabilities: Provides first-run and main runtime commands for hosted text-to-image generation.
Supports model selection with documented examples using openai.gpt-image-2.
Uses cost limits through a maximum estimated USD per image option.
Returns durable hosted media URLs, recoverable jobs, and cost receipts through the Image Skill runtime.
Documents a no-spend guide path before creating paid media.
Includes installation commands for Codex-compatible skill environments.
limitations: Requires network access to the Image Skill hosted service and related documentation.
Uses remote npx packages, so users should review package provenance and version changes.
May require spend approval, credits, or payment flow before creating images.
Does not provide a local image model or offline generation path.

use_cases.title: Product concept visuals
use_cases.description: Create hosted product image drafts from short briefs while keeping cost estimates visible.

use_cases.title: Campaign illustration drafts
use_cases.description: Generate prompt-based illustration options for articles, launch pages, and social assets.

use_cases.title: Agent-created design artifacts
use_cases.description: Let coding agents create durable image assets that future sessions can recover and cite.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for one clear generated asset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a hosted image of [subject] in [style]. Keep the prompt simple and use the no-spend guide before creating media.

prompt_templates.title: Explore visual directions
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare several creative options before committing to a final prompt.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create image concepts for [project]. Try three distinct visual directions and explain which prompt should be generated first.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a constrained asset
prompt_templates.scenario: Request an image with clear format, subject, and budget constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a [format] image for [use case] showing [subject]. Use a maximum estimated cost of [amount] per image.

prompt_templates.title: Recover and document output
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for durable assets and audit-friendly records.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a hosted image for [brief], preserve the job details, cite the media URL, and summarize the cost receipt and model parameters.

output_examples.input: A request for a compact field camera on a stainless workbench.
output_examples.output: A hosted image result with a durable media URL, job details, selected model, and cost receipt summary.

output_examples.input: A request for three product illustration directions for a launch page.
output: A short comparison of visual directions.
A reminder to use the no-spend guide before paid creation.

output_examples.input: A request to continue work from a previous image-generation job.
output_examples.output: A recovered hosted job reference with the previous media location and guidance for the next generation step.

best_practices: Run the no-spend guide first and create paid media only after explicit spend approval.
Use precise prompts that name subject, setting, style, and intended use.
Record hosted media URLs, model parameters, and cost receipt details for future recovery.
anti_patterns: Do not paste private customer data, unreleased designs, or secrets into image prompts.
Do not run remote package commands in restricted environments without reviewing provenance.
Do not bypass the cost limit or guide flow when paid media creation is uncertain.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the Image Skill hosted runtime and requires network access.

faq.question: Does it require provider API keys?
faq.answer: The skill says provider API keys are not needed for the hosted workflow.

faq.question: Can it create paid images?
faq.answer: Yes, but the skill directs agents to use the guide first and create media only when spend is allowed.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What is the main security warning?
faq.answer: The workflow runs remote npx packages and sends work to hosted image-skill endpoints.

faq.question: Are the static crypto warnings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The cited lines contain prose, not weak cryptographic code.
